stories.headline: Senate impeachment court issues pre-trial order ahead of Sara Duterte trial
stories.summary: The Senate impeachment court transmitted its pre-trial order to both sides on June 29, clerk of court Renato Bantug Jr. said, with the document emailed to Vice President Sara Duterte's defense team and the House prosecution panel late Monday afternoon and printed copies to follow. According to Bantug, the order sets out the admitted evidence and witnesses, the trial timeline and number of hearing days, and the sequence in which the impeachment articles will be taken up. He said both parties have three days to file replies before the trial opens on July 6.
stories.context: Duterte faces four articles of impeachment, including alleged misuse of confidential funds and an alleged threat against the lives of President Marcos and other officials; the charges remain allegations to be tried.
stories.watch_for: The trial is scheduled to begin July 6; the parties have three days to file replies to the pre-trial order.

stories.headline: Ombudsman to file plunder case against Senator Marcoleta over 2025 campaign donations
stories.summary: Ombudsman Jesus Crispin Remulla said his office will file a non-bailable plunder case before the Sandiganbayan against Senator Rodante Marcoleta and three others over donations it says totaled about P75 million for his 2025 senatorial campaign. The Ombudsman said former lawmaker Mike Defensor, Aristotle Viray and Joseph Espiritu allegedly gave amounts exceeding the P50-million anti-plunder threshold that were not reflected in Marcoleta's wealth declaration. Marcoleta said the donations were private campaign funds spent before his declaration was prepared. The allegations remain to be tried.

stories.headline: Ex-public works chief Bonoan to turn state witness in flood-control plunder case
stories.summary: Ombudsman Jesus Crispin Remulla said his office signed a cooperation agreement for former Public Works Secretary Manuel Bonoan to become a state witness, saying his knowledge of the department would aid several prosecutions. The agreement relates to a plunder charge before the Sandiganbayan in which Senator Jinggoy Estrada is a co-accused, over alleged irregular budget insertions and project allocations at the department totaling P573 million for 2025. Prosecutors moved to discharge Bonoan from the case. The allegations remain to be tried.
stories.context: Bonoan was arrested on June 2 amid investigations into alleged anomalies in government flood-control projects.
stories.watch_for: Bonoan and Estrada were scheduled for arraignment on June 30.

stories.headline: Defense chief rebukes Chinese embassy over rejection of South China Sea ruling
stories.summary: Defense Secretary Gilberto Teodoro Jr. criticized the Chinese Embassy in Manila after it dismissed the Philippines' reaffirmation of the 2016 South China Sea arbitral award, the Department of National Defense said. The embassy had called the ruling 'political manipulation disguised in legal garb' on June 23. Teodoro said the response revealed Beijing's intentions and described it as an arrogant display, according to the department. The exchange comes ahead of the July anniversary of the award, which rejected China's expansive claims; Beijing continues to reject it.
stories.context: An arbitral tribunal in The Hague ruled in July 2016 that China's sweeping South China Sea claims had no legal basis, a decision Beijing has never recognized.
stories.watch_for: The 10th anniversary of the arbitral award falls in mid-July.

stories.headline: Lawmakers seek inquiry into alleged US e-waste shipments to Subic
stories.summary: Makabayan bloc lawmakers filed House Resolution 1164 seeking an investigation into the alleged illegal shipment of 235 containers of hazardous electronic and plastic waste from the United States to the Subic Bay Freeport, ABS-CBN News reported. Kabataan Rep. Renee Co said improper disposal of e-waste could harm health and the environment and that it is classified as toxic. Co said the shipments would breach the Basel Convention on hazardous waste, which the Philippines has signed, and cited the Basel Action Network, which she said flagged such shipments to customs 14 times since March 2025.

stories.headline: Marcos administration proposes P7.2-trillion national budget for 2027
stories.summary: The Marcos administration is proposing a P7.2-trillion national budget for 2027, the Inquirer reported, citing a memorandum signed by acting Budget Secretary Kim Robert de Leon. The figure is about P407 billion, or roughly 6 percent, higher than the 2026 budget of P6.79 trillion and equal to about 21.7 percent of projected economic output. The budget department cited limited fiscal space due to automatically appropriated items and rising debt-service costs, with interest payments projected at P950 billion. The government has lowered its 2026 growth target amid economic headwinds.

stories.headline: Fuel prices set to rise on June 30 after weeks of Middle East-driven swings
stories.summary: Oil firms will raise pump prices effective Tuesday, June 30, with Pilipinas Shell announcing increases of P1.65 per liter for gasoline, P0.84 for diesel and P1.22 for kerosene, GMA News Online reported. The report attributed the movement to steadier global oil prices following developments in the Middle East, including a reported agreement between Iran and the United States to halt recent hostilities in the Gulf. Energy Secretary Sharon Garin was quoted as saying prices were beginning to settle rather than swing sharply.

stories.headline: Finance chief promotes investment in Camarines Sur offshore wind project
stories.summary: Finance Secretary Frederick Go urged investment in the San Miguel Bay Offshore Wind Power Project in Camarines Sur, which GMA News Online reported is set to become Southeast Asia's first offshore wind farm with a planned capacity of 901 megawatts. The project is a partnership involving Copenhagen Infrastructure Partners, ACEN Corporation and Offshore Wind Philippines. Go said offshore wind is important to long-term energy security and sustainability goals. According to GMA, it aligns with the country's energy plan through 2050 and aims to add cleaner power, create jobs and meet rising demand.

stories.headline: STAR Tollway and SLEX toll rates to increase on July 1
stories.summary: Motorists using the STAR Tollway and the South Luzon Expressway will pay higher fees from Wednesday, July 1, as the second phase of approved rate adjustments takes effect, ABS-CBN News reported. The Toll Regulatory Board said Class 1 vehicles on the STAR Tollway's Sto. Tomas–Lipa section will pay P64, up by P4, with larger increases for heavier vehicles, while Class 1 vehicles on SLEX's Alabang–Calamba section will pay P137 after an P11 increase. According to the report, the adjustments were divided into two phases to lessen the impact on motorists.

stories.headline: Two workers killed in Las Piñas excavation collapse at water project site
stories.summary: Two construction workers were killed and one was injured when a deep excavation collapsed on the afternoon of June 28 in Barangay Pamplona Dos, Las Piñas City, Manila Bulletin reported. According to the report, five workers were digging at a Maynilad pump station site about 18 meters deep when the lower portion of the concrete shaft gave way, trapping three under soil and debris. City disaster responders and firefighters rescued the injured worker and later recovered the two who died. The report said authorities sealed the area pending an investigation into the cause.

stories.headline: Experts warn pollutants from Navotas landfill fire could linger for years
stories.summary: Experts warned that pollutants released by the Navotas Sanitary Landfill fire, which broke out on April 10 and burned for about a month before being put out, could persist in the environment for years, the Inquirer reported. Specialists from the University of the Philippines and the Ateneo were cited warning about dioxins and furans, among the most hazardous organic pollutants, along with methane and sulfur dioxide. They said short-term exposure can trigger asthma attacks and irritation, while long-term exposure may contribute to chronic illnesses, with children and the elderly most at risk.

stories.headline: Mandaluyong City confirms local mpox case, begins contact tracing
stories.summary: Mandaluyong City's epidemiology and surveillance unit said on June 29 that it is handling a local case of mpox, the disease formerly known as monkeypox, and has begun case investigation, contact tracing and monitoring of close contacts, the city said in an advisory. Officials said they coordinated with local and regional health offices and urged residents to rely on official channels, avoid sharing unverified information and respect the patient's privacy; no further details were released. Citing the WHO, Philippine Star reported that mpox is usually mild.

stories.headline: Quezon City puts comprehensive road safety code into full effect
stories.summary: Quezon City Mayor Joy Belmonte on June 29 led the launch of the implementing rules for the city's Comprehensive Road Safety Code and Safe School Zone Ordinance, putting the 2018 code into full force, Philippine Star reported, citing the Philippine News Agency. The city described the code as the first local measure in the country to adopt a 'Safe System' approach to road design. Belmonte said trained assessors will audit school road safety, and the ordinance requires lower speed limits and better pedestrian infrastructure near schools.

stories.headline: Four wounded in police clash with armed group in Arayat, Pampanga
stories.summary: Four people, including a former rebel, were wounded in a clash with police in Barangay San Juan Baño, Arayat, Pampanga, on the night of June 28 after residents reported indiscriminate gunfire, Manila Bulletin reported. According to PNP chief Gen. Jose Melencio Nartatez Jr., one of the group opened fire when officers told them to surrender, prompting an exchange of gunfire; no officers were hurt. The report said one of the wounded was a former communist rebel who surrendered in 2018 and led a local armed group, and that police recovered several firearms and a grenade.

stories.headline: Anti-drug agents seize P13.6-million shabu haul in Maguindanao del Norte
stories.summary: Anti-drug agents arrested a 42-year-old man in a buy-bust operation in Parang, Maguindanao del Norte, and seized about two kilograms of suspected shabu worth roughly P13.6 million, the Philippine Drug Enforcement Agency's Bangsamoro office said. Regional director Edgar Jubay told reporters on June 29 that the suspect allegedly sold the drugs to agents and that a vehicle allegedly used to transport narcotics was also seized, according to Philippine Star. Jubay said charges under the dangerous drugs law are being prepared. The allegations remain to be tried.

stories.headline: PAGASA monitors low-pressure area that may become a tropical cyclone
stories.summary: PAGASA said on June 29 that a low-pressure area about 390 kilometers east of Guiuan, Eastern Samar had a high chance of developing into a tropical cyclone within 24 hours, Manila Bulletin reported. According to weather specialist Aldczar Aurelio, the system was expected to move across Southern Luzon and the Visayas on June 30 before emerging over the West Philippine Sea around July 1. The agency said it would bring scattered rains and thunderstorms over the Visayas, Bicol, Caraga and parts of Mindanao. PAGASA said it would be named 'Henry' if it becomes a cyclone within Philippine waters.
stories.watch_for: The system is forecast to cross Southern Luzon and the Visayas on June 30 and could intensify after reaching the West Philippine Sea around July 1.

stories.headline: Philippine men's football team qualifies for 2026 Asian Games
stories.summary: The Philippine men's national football team has qualified for the 20th Asian Games in Aichi and Nagoya, Japan, after India withdrew and left the Philippines next in line for the slot, the Philippine Olympic Committee announced on June 29, according to ABS-CBN Sports. POC President Abraham Tolentino said the addition brings the country's delegation to 443 athletes, with the women's team having qualified earlier, and that Filipinos will compete in 38 of the 47 sports on the program. Tolentino said the delegation aims to improve on its four-gold finish at the 2023 edition in Hangzhou.
stories.watch_for: The 2026 Asian Games run from September 19 to October 4.

stories.headline: US and Iran agree to halt strikes around Strait of Hormuz, reports say
stories.summary: The United States and Iran have agreed to 'stand down' after days of reciprocal strikes tied to their dispute over the Strait of Hormuz, the BBC reported, citing a US official. The official said vessels would again pass through the strait freely and that talks to end the conflict would continue; Iran had not publicly confirmed any halt. The flare-up followed a June 17 memorandum that came under strain after an Iranian projectile struck a cargo ship and US forces retaliated. Iran said it struck US bases in Kuwait and Bahrain, which Washington said caused no casualties.
stories.context: The Strait of Hormuz is a key route for global oil shipments, and disruptions there can affect world fuel prices, including in the Philippines.

stories.headline: Venezuela earthquake death toll passes 1,400 as search continues
stories.summary: At least 1,450 people have been killed by twin earthquakes that struck Venezuela's coastal La Guaira region, the BBC reported, with tens of thousands still reported missing and the country's interim president describing it as Venezuela's most severe natural disaster. The BBC said search efforts continued over the weekend but that hopes of finding more survivors were fading. It cited a mother who said she and her newborn were pulled from the rubble of their eighth-floor apartment after being trapped when the quakes struck. The casualty figures were attributed to Venezuelan authorities.

stories.headline: Israel strikes southern Lebanon, testing days-old ceasefire framework
stories.summary: Israel carried out air strikes on southern Lebanon on June 28, two days after signing a US-brokered ceasefire framework intended to end a war that began in March, Al Jazeera reported. The framework ties an Israeli withdrawal to the disarmament of Hezbollah; Lebanese authorities have reported more than 4,200 deaths since March, a figure attributed to them. Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu called the deal historic, while Defense Minister Israel Katz said forces were preparing to remain in a buffer zone. Hezbollah leader Naim Qassem rejected the framework as a surrender of sovereignty.

stories.headline: Australia and Vanuatu sign pact barring foreign military bases
stories.summary: Australia and Vanuatu signed an agreement under which Australia pledges greater economic support and Vanuatu agrees that no foreign military base will be established on its territory, Deutsche Welle reported, describing it as a move to limit China's growing security role in the Pacific. Prime Minister Anthony Albanese, who signed the deal in Canberra with Vanuatu's Jotham Napat, said it ensures there will be no foreign base on the islands. According to DW, the deal also names Australia as Vanuatu's main policing partner and covers maritime, cyber and intelligence cooperation.
stories.context: China is Vanuatu's largest external creditor and has expanded its presence in the Pacific, raising concern in Canberra and Washington about a potential naval base.

stories.headline: Japan to launch eight digital infrastructure projects across Indo-Pacific
stories.summary: Japan's communications ministry will launch eight infrastructure projects across the Indo-Pacific, in countries from Taiwan to India, to strengthen economic-security ties in areas such as undersea cables and satellite communications, Nikkei Asia reported. The report said the effort is part of an expanded 'free and open Indo-Pacific' approach outlined by Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi. According to Nikkei, the plan responds to digital infrastructure becoming more critical with the spread of artificial intelligence and to intensifying geopolitical competition in the region.
